name: Josephine Baker
description: American-born French dancer, singer and actress (1906–1975)
abstract: Freda Josephine Baker (née McDonald; June 3, 1906 – April 12, 1975), also spelled Joséphine Baker, was an American-born French dancer, singer, actress, and spy for the French Resistance. Her career was centered primarily in Europe, mostly in France.

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Clarke
about_quotes.evidence: they said there hadn't been any colored musicians there since Josephine Baker, which was about 25 years before.

about_quotes.speaker: Sherrie Maricle
about_quotes.evidence: he was the drummer with Josephine Baker... and he managed the Buddy Rich band

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: So from about, oh, about four months we toured with Josephine Baker

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] That's the show they had Josephine Baker, wasn't it?

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: she suggested that we do a little act together... We rehearsed it and got it down very well. And she would go around me and dance while I would play the trumpet. And it became part of the show.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Stanley Kay
about_quotes.evidence: But we toured with Josephine. It was Sweets Edison, Buddy and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Stanley Kay interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001
